arts.ttl: Jack Grealish is left OUT of Man City's squad to face Newcastle - two days after he was seen 'worse for wear' in late-night exit from A-list hotspot
arts.sum: Jack Grealish was left out of Man City's squad for their game against Newcastle on Saturday, just two days after a late-night out from an A-list hotspot in London.

arts.ttl: Manchester City vs Newcastle – Match Preview and team news
arts.sum: Manchester City battle it out with Newcastle United this afternoon with the result potentially of great significance. The two sides are level in the table with 41 points apiece and the winner of the contest would be in an advantageous position to secure Champions League qualification. arts.ttl: Newcastle United spring a surprise as £38m man returns to the bench and full-backs tweaked
arts.sum: Howe was also able to recall Dan Burn to the heart of his defence with the Blyth lad recovered from a groin injury suffered at Birmingham City in the FA Cup fourth round win last weekend. Burn partnered Fabian Schar in the heart of defence while Kieran Trippier started the game at right-back at the expense of Tino Livramento.

arts.ttl: Arsenal, Newcastle and Villa looking to raid top-four rivals for €50m-rated forward
arts.sum: Arsenal, Newcastle United and Aston Villa are keen on signing the Chelsea striker Nicolas Jackson at the end of the season. The former Villarreal striker has shown improvement this season and he has done reasonably well for the Blues. According to a report from Superdeporte via SportWitness, the three English clubs are hoping to secure […] The post Arsenal, Newcastle and Villa looking to raid top-four rivals for €50m-rated forward appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Newcastle's smart move as Man City expose panicked email amid 'open warfare'
arts.sum: This latest legal battle comes after the tribunal ruled the previous APT regulations were 'void and unenforceable' and Newcastle's takeover proved a crucial reference point in Manchester City's argument. "I suspect that was the primary motive but there's also potentially a secondary motive when it comes to these types of actions and that is to send a warning shot out to the Premier League and its clubs that they [Manchester City] are prepared to use every avenue available, including through legal proceedings, to protect their interests and what they perceive to be the unfairness and unlawfulness of the rules.
